episode_title: Alessandro Alviani (Part One): Building AI Products with an Editor-Centric Approach
transcript_file: 2024-02-09 - Alessandro Alviani (Part One) Building AI Products with an Editor-Centric Approach.txt
summary_claims: IPPEN started AI workshops for editors on November 29, 2022 -- exactly one day before the launch of ChatGPT -- demonstrating they were already investing in AI education before the generative AI hype
Replacing human journalists with AI systems at Microsoft News had negative consequences both for the people directly involved and for the quality of news coverage
The risk of editorial, stylistic, and grammatical errors is becoming bigger especially at weekends and in the evening due to time pressure
Editor-centric product development where editors are involved at all stages leads to tools that bring real business value and match editor workflows

matches.theme_name: The Automation Paradox: What AI Replaces, What Remains Irreplaceable

best_quote.text: I've been in a situation where I had to tell my colleagues, I'm sorry, we have to cancel your contract. And I've seen what happens when you replace human journalists with AI systems and have seen which kind of consequences this can have... for the people who are directly involved but also for the quality of the news coverage.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: This episode is deeply concerned with the human-AI labor division. Alviani provides firsthand testimony of job displacement at Microsoft News, contrasting it with Ippen's editor-centric model where AI augments rather than replaces journalists. The range of automated tasks (transcription, summarization, headline generation, illustration, style-checking) is extensive, while human editorial judgment remains the gatekeeper through human-in-the-loop principles.
matches.matched_text: I've been in a situation where I had to tell my colleagues And please be aware of the fact I was not a Microsoft employee. I was employed by a vendor. I was in the situation of telling my colleagues, I'm sorry, we have to cancel your contract. And I've seen what happens when you replace human journalists with AI systems and have seen which kind of consequences this can have and for the people who are directly involved involved but also for the quality of the news coverage.

matches.theme_name: Trust on the Line: Credibility, Transparency, and the Audience Contract

best_quote.text: When we talk about transparency, we mean both internal transparency and external transparency. Internal transparency means basically that we developed a series... to talk regularly with our editors... and external transparency means bylines.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Trust and transparency are foundational to Alviani's approach. He articulates a dual transparency framework: internal (editor education and discussion) and external (bylines disclosing AI use to readers). Trust in news media is positioned as critically important in the generative AI era, with human-in-the-loop as a trust preservation mechanism.
matches.matched_text: when we talk about transparency, we mean both internal transparency and external transparency. Internal transparency means basically that we developed a series. I already mentioned the AI workshops. from our internal academy, but additional, we have developed additional formats to talk regularly with our editors and discuss potential Applications and external transparency means bylines.

matches.theme_name: The Ethics Infrastructure: From Principles on Paper to Practice in the Newsroom

best_quote.text: When we talk about responsible use, I talk about the fact that if something goes wrong and it can happen, unfortunately, then it's not the algorithm's fault that something has been published, that it was false, but it's our own. We are accountable for that.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Ethics is deeply operationalized at Ippen: guardrails established before development begins, human-in-the-loop as a core principle, accountability placed on the newsroom rather than the algorithm, and a deliberate illustration-only policy for AI images to avoid photorealistic deception. This represents a mature ethics-to-practice pipeline.
matches.matched_text: when we talk about responsible use, I talk about the fact that if something goes wrong And it can happen, unfortunately. Then it's not the algorithm's fault that something has been published, that it was false, but it's our own. We are accountable for that. And transparency is a key challenge to us.

matches.theme_name: Building the Machine: Technical Architecture and Engineering Decisions

best_quote.text: We have developed a system to self-evaluate the output of an LLM, meaning basically a system that is able to assist journalists when it comes to evaluate the accuracy of a piece of content that has been edited with the output of an LLM.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: The LLM self-evaluation system with metrics (correctness, completeness) and automatic reprompting is a notable technical contribution. Fine-tuning open-source models on Ippen's articles is mentioned as a direction. The architecture of tools integrated into the CMS and the prototyping platform for multiple Gen AI tasks reflects sophisticated technical choices.
matches.matched_text: we have developed a prototyping platform. For a wide range of Gen AI tasks. So editors can, for example, transcribe interviews, summarize articles, rephrase articles in plain language, or generate video scripts. That can be later augmented with synthetic voices for AI-powered video explainers.

matches.theme_name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling the Newsroom for an AI Era

best_quote.text: I think it's our responsibility to help editors to develop a more realistic approach to LLMs.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Ippen's three-pronged upskilling approach is exemplary: an internal academy with workshops (started one day before ChatGPT launch), dedicated two-week internships where editors embed with the AI team, and product discovery sessions with deep-dive interviews. This is structured, experiential AI literacy that counters LinkedIn hype with realistic understanding.
matches.matched_text: I think it's our responsibility to help editors To develop a more realistic approach to LLMs.

matches.theme_name: Organizing for AI: Leadership, Strategy, and Institutional Transformation

best_quote.text: They are not just users. They are co-creators.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Ippen's organizational model is extensively described: a 10-person cross-functional AI team with editors and developers, editor-centric product development where editors are co-creators, product discovery sessions that visit editors in different cities, standardization as a scaling principle, impact-effort prioritization metrics, and alignment with company OKRs. The 'journalist-first' identity is embodied in Alviani's own career trajectory.
matches.matched_text: This is Newsroom Robots, the podcast where we explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and the news industry. I'm Nikita Roy, data scientist, media entrepreneur, and one of the many founders currently building their. Ventures at the Harvard Innovation Labs. On the Newsroom Robots, I'm excited to bring you insightful conversations with industry experts about how AI is impacting the way we do journalism. With me today is Alessandro Albiani, the product lead for AI at the German newsroom Eppen Digital. He's been a fellow of the 2022 Journalism AI Fellowship Programme at the London School of Economics. And has also previously worked as the editorial coordinator at the Microsoft News Hub in Berlin. I'm excited to kick off a two-part episode with Alessandro this week. In part one, he shares his editor-centric approach to AI product development and discusses the range of generative AI products.

matches.theme_name: Power, Money, and the Platform: Big Tech and the News Industry

best_quote.text: I've been there. I've been in a situation where I had to tell my colleagues, I'm sorry, we have to cancel your contract.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: The Microsoft News replacement of human journalists with AI curation is a cautionary tale about big tech platform power over newsroom labor, but it serves primarily as backstory for Alviani's editor-centric philosophy rather than a sustained platform power analysis.
matches.matched_text: I've been there. I've been in a situation where I had to tell my colleagues And please be aware of the fact I was not a Microsoft employee.

matches.theme_name: Reimagining the News Product: Personalization, Formats, and the Experience Layer

best_quote.text: We recently launched an AI feature in our apps to allow readers to read articles in different formats. So we started with bullet points, but for example, later on you can... translate this article into another language, or for example, translate it into plain language, simple language.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Ippen's reader-facing app feature that lets users choose content formats (bullet points, summaries, translations, plain language) is a concrete personalization product. The illustration-only AI image policy and synthetic voice video explainers represent format innovation.
matches.matched_text: we recently launched an AI feature in our apps to allow readers to read articles in different formats. So we started with bullet points, but for example, later on you can. Let's assume you have just one minute before you have the next meeting you can generate bullet points but if you have a bit more time you can read a longer summary can even Translate this article into another language, or for example, translate it into plain language, simple language, and so on.

matches.theme_name: Survival Economics: Business Models, Revenue, and Market Position in the AI Era

best_quote.text: We work with OKRs, for example, and we use frameworks and we implement frameworks that were already there to make sure that those products are basically aligned with the goals, the overall goals of the company.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Business value is mentioned as a requirement for AI tools (must 'create business value' and align with company strategy), and Ippen is described as a large German media corporation with local news outlets, but specific revenue models are not the focus.
matches.matched_text: This is Newsroom Robots, the podcast where we explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and the news industry. I'm Nikita Roy, data scientist, media entrepreneur, and one of the many founders currently building their. Ventures at the Harvard Innovation Labs. On the Newsroom Robots, I'm excited to bring you insightful conversations with industry experts about how AI is impacting the way we do journalism. With me today is Alessandro Albiani, the product lead for AI at the German newsroom Eppen Digital. He's been a fellow of the 2022 Journalism AI Fellowship Programme at the London School of Economics. And has also previously worked as the editorial coordinator at the Microsoft News Hub in Berlin. I'm excited to kick off a two-part episode with Alessandro this week. In part one, he shares his editor-centric approach to AI product development and discusses the range of generative AI products.

matches.theme_name: Regulating the Revolution: Law, Policy, and Journalism as Accountability

best_quote.text: We do not create images that could be mistaken for photos. So we only produce illustration style pictures because they are instantly recognizable as AI generated pictures.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: While Ippen's internal policies function as self-regulation (illustration-only image policy, transparency bylines), formal external regulatory frameworks are not discussed.
matches.matched_text: We do not create images that could be mistaken for photos. So we only produce illustration style Pictures because they are instantly recognizable as AI generated pictures.

matches.theme_name: The Prototype Pipeline: From Experiment to Shipped Product

best_quote.text: We usually don't ask them, what do you want to have? Because this will be a bit tricky question, but we try to better understand how they work and later analyze their feedback so we have a better idea of their work day and their pain points.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: The product discovery methodology (deep-dive interviews with editors, understanding workflows before building) and the iterative development cycle (editorial assistant co-developed with interns, transcription tool features driven by editor feedback) demonstrate a mature prototype-to-production pipeline. Prioritization uses standardization, impact-effort, feasibility, and speed criteria.
matches.matched_text: We usually don't ask them, what do you want to have? Because this will be a bit tricky question, but we try to better understand how they work and later analyze their feedback so we have a better idea of their work day. and their pain points, and we address, we try to address those pain points and needs with AI powered tools.

matches.theme_name: Data Privacy, Source Protection, and the Boundaries of Responsible Use

best_quote.text: We started experimenting on a variety of use cases with several partners, including Aleph Alpha, a German company that develops its own LLMs.
best_quote.speaker: Alessandro Alviani

matches.summary: Data practices are touched on through the use of Ippen's own article corpus for fine-tuning and the partnership with German LLM company Aleph Alpha, but data governance and privacy are not substantively addressed.
matches.matched_text: We started experimenting on a variety of use cases with several partners, including Alefalfa, a German company that develops its own LLMs. That means that when HGBT came out, we were basically, we had already collected a lot of experience on how to develop tools based on LLMs and most importantly we started building a knowledge base for our editors to empower them.
